text.title: Playwright Patterns
text.summary: Launch, auto-waiting, locators, form handling, network interception, and auth state persistence.

rule.statement: Use role= and text= locators before CSS selectors; never use CSS class-based selectors in automation scripts.
rule.rationale: CSS class selectors break on every UI refactor, especially with Tailwind JIT; accessibility role and text selectors are refactor-stable.

rule.statement: Never use page.waitForTimeout(N); always use condition-based waits (waitForSelector, waitForResponse, waitForURL, expect.poll).
rule.rationale: Fixed timeouts are either too short (flaky) or too long (slow); condition-based waits complete as soon as the condition is met.

rule.statement: Persist auth state via context.storageState({ path }) and reuse it across test sessions; never re-run the login flow in every test.
rule.rationale: Login flows are fragile and slow; running them per-test multiplies flakiness and execution time.

rule.statement: Block images, fonts, and analytics scripts on all scraping runs; they add latency and bandwidth without data value.
rule.rationale: Blocking non-essential resources reduces page load time by 3-5x and drops bandwidth ~80%.

text.reference: Playwright extractor with Zod schema validation
